data.description: A creature can't be a construct or undead. You must have seen at least an example of the sort of creature you wish to transform into without a class or Spellcasting trait. The statistics of the chosen creature are used, though you retain your alignment and Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma scores. Also, all your skill proficiencies remain. If the transformed form has the same proficiency bonus as yours, it protects you. Until the start of your next turn, you have a +5 AC bonus and gain advantage on saving throws against magic missile. The use of the creature's statistics replaces yours, but you can't make attacks or take actions in the new form. When you revert to your normal form, you return to the number of hit points you had before transforming. If you revert as a result of dropping to 0 hit points, any excess damage carried over doesn't carry over when you return to normal.

1st-level abjuration
Casting Time: reaction, you when are targeted by the magic missile spell hit an or Range: Self
Components: V,S
Duration: 1 round

You extend your hand, and lightning springs from it to deliver a shock to a creature you can see within range. You make a melee attack roll against the target. If the target is wearing metal armor or carrying a metal weapon, you have advantage on the attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 lightning damage, and until the start of your next turn, it can't take reactions.
